sections.paragraph: or תָּן only in plur. תַּנִּים and תַּנִּין m. a certain beast dwelling in deserts, Isa 13:22 43:20 34:13 (whence מְקֹום תַּנִּים Psa 44:20 and מְעֹון תַּנִּים Jerem. 9:10 10:22 49:33 , used of the desert); it suckles its young, Lam 4:3 and utters a mournful cry, Job 30:29 Mic 1:8. Bochart (Hieroz. ii. p. 429) takes it to be great serpents, as if it were the same as תַּנִין, but amongst the Hebrews, R. Tanchum of Jerusalem correctly explains this word ابن اوى jackal, wild dog, so called from its cry (elsewhere Hebr. אִי plur. אִיִּים), compare Arab. تنَانُ wolf, both from the root תָּנַן No. 2.
